chunks.en: I usually run the washing machine at night, about three or four times a week.
chunks.ja: 私はたいてい夜に洗濯機を回していて、週に三、四回くらいです。

chunks.en: Before I start, I sort my clothes and pick a gentle cycle for delicate stuff.
chunks.ja: 始める前に服を分けて、デリケートなものには弱い（優しい）コースを選びます。

chunks.en: When it comes to drying, I usually hang everything indoors since it rains a lot.
chunks.ja: 乾かすことに関しては、雨が多いのでたいてい部屋干しにしています。

chunks.en: To be honest, I always put off folding the laundry until the pile gets huge.
chunks.ja: 正直なところ、洗濯物をたたむのは山が大きくなるまでいつも後回しにしてしまいます。

outline: Opening: when and how often I do laundry
Point 1: sorting clothes and choosing a setting
Wrap-up: folding and my habit of putting it off

modelTalk.text: I usually run the washing machine at night, after dinner. It's quieter then, and the clothes are ready by morning. I do about three loads a week, sometimes four if we're busy. Before I start, I sort things out. I keep the whites away from anything bright, because colours can bleed. For a normal load I pick a quick, cool wash to save energy. If something's really dirty, though, I'll bump up the temperature. We don't have a dryer, so I hang everything on a rack indoors. In winter it takes ages, maybe a whole day to dry. On sunny days, I put it outside and it's done in a couple of hours. Then comes the part I hate: folding. Honestly, I always put it off. The clean pile just sits there, staring at me. Eventually I give in, fold it all, and finally tuck it away in the drawers.

chunks.en: So, to be honest, I usually run the washing machine at night after I get home from work.
chunks.ja: 正直に言うと、仕事から帰ってきた後、夜に洗濯機を回すことが多いんです。

chunks.en: The main thing I focus on is sorting my dark clothes from the whites first.
chunks.ja: 僕が一番気をつけているのは、まず色物と白物を分けることですね。

chunks.en: What works well for me is drying everything indoors, since it usually takes half a day.
chunks.ja: 僕にとってうまくいくのは部屋干しで、だいたい半日くらいで乾くんです。

chunks.en: That said, I'll admit that folding and putting things away is what I tend to put off.
chunks.ja: とはいえ、正直たたんでしまう作業は、つい後回しにしがちだと認めます。

outline: Opening: when I run the machine
Drying indoors, takes half a day
Wrap-up: folding is what I put off

modelTalk.text:
So, when do I actually do my laundry? Honestly, I'm a night owl, so I usually run the machine after dinner. I do maybe three loads a week, give or take. That said, I try not to leave wet clothes sitting overnight.

Before I start, I sort everything — darks in one pile, whites in another. I pick a gentle cycle for delicate stuff, and I keep the water cool to stop colours running.

Drying? Well, my flat doesn't get much sun, so I hang things up indoors on a rack. It takes ages, to be honest — usually a full day. Sometimes I'll cheat and use the dryer if I'm in a rush.

Folding is where I fall down, though. I tend to dump everything on a chair and put it off. Eventually the pile gets so big I can't ignore it. Then I finally fold it all and stash it away properly.
